event_summary: Ukraine launched one of its largest drone barrages on Moscow on 17–18 May 2026, killing at least four people; Russia retaliated with a new air barrage across Ukraine, and a Russian drone struck a Chinese cargo ship in the Black Sea on the eve of Putin's Beijing summit.
tldr: SECURITY_DEFENSE is the dominant headline frame in 16 of 23 buckets — drone exchanges, casualty counts, and strike escalation. The most notable headline-layer sub-story is the Russian drone strike on a Chinese cargo ship, which appears prominently in pakistan, uk, ukraine, vietnam_thai_my, and wire_services, creating a POLITICAL sub-frame around Putin-Xi summit timing. CRIME_PUNISHMENT headlines (balkans shadow fleet, south_africa corruption, iran_opposition graft) constitute a distinct third thread. Nordic is the most isolated bucket (0.343) because Yle framed the story entirely through Stubb's diplomatic call to Zelensky. Turkey's Anadolu headline is a hantavirus article misrouted into this corpus — the only outright cross-story bleed in ukraine_war today. Pan_african's sole headline covers only the prisoner swap, the one humanitarian angle absent from the combat-dominant majority.

evidence.quote: Ukraine hits Russia with fatal drone attack
Ukraine's Biggest Assault On Moscow Region In A Year Leaves 3 Dead
Russia launches new air barrage on Ukraine
Russia and Ukraine exchange overnight drone attacks

frames.sub_frame: Chinese ship, Putin-Xi timing, peace talks

evidence.quote: 'strikes Chinese cargo ship' day before Putin-Xi summit
Russian Drone Reportedly Hits Chinese Ship as Zelensky Warns of Wider Escalation
Drone strike near DPRK embassy dims prospects
Evika Silina resigns over handling of drone incidents
Prosecutor Seeks Detention Of Zelenskyy's Ex-Chief Of Staff
Former top Zelensky aide detained over money laundering
accused of putting activists at risk

frames.sub_frame: Russian media distortion, war support divides

evidence.quote: Russian state TV channels played down scale of Ukraine's strike
Most citizens consider government's foreign policy poor

isolation_top.note: Yle headline about Stubb's Zelensky call frames the war through Finnish presidential diplomacy — a political-engagement angle semantically distant from the drone-barrage dominant.
AfricaNews ran only the 205-for-205 prisoner-swap headline — the sole title not about drones, strikes, or politics; humanitarian-exchange focus pulls it far from the combat-count majority.
News24 headlined the Zelensky aide money-laundering detention — a CRIME_PUNISHMENT angle with significant semantic distance from frontline operational reporting.
Vatican Nuncio headline frames heavy attacks as evidence of Russia's intention to continue the war — moral-inference language distinct from operational strike-count reporting.

Taipei Times headline ('Barrage of Ukraine drones kills four people in Russia') is factual but very brief (12 LaBSE tokens), limiting embedding resolution against longer multi-clause headlines in the corpus.

silences.what_they_covered_instead: AfricaNews covered only the Ukraine-Russia prisoner swap — a humanitarian-exchange angle that leaves the dominant drone-escalation and diplomatic-crisis frames absent from the Africa title layer.

Deutsche Welle's first headline covers German chemical-industry decline (an economic-spillover story) rather than frontline Ukraine war developments; only the Latvia PM resignation headline connects to the conflict.

single_outlet_findings.finding: Turkey's second headline ('Hantavirus cruise ship returns to the Netherlands for quarantine, deep cleaning') is a hantavirus article misrouted into the ukraine_war corpus — the clearest cross-story bleed in today's ukraine_war briefing.

Only outlet reporting that Russian state TV deliberately played down the scale of Ukraine's largest Moscow assault in over a year — a media-manipulation angle absent from every other bucket and the sharpest accountability headline in the corpus.

Nuncio to Ukraine headline ('Heavy attacks suggest no intention to end war') infers political intent from military action — a moral-framing register absent from all other buckets and a clean MORALITY/SECURITY_DEFENSE hybrid not captured by any other title.

bottom_line: Ukraine's drone barrage on Moscow produces tight SECURITY_DEFENSE consensus across 16 buckets, but the headline margins carry distinct sub-stories: the Chinese cargo ship strike generates a POLITICAL layer across five buckets tied to Putin-Xi summit timing, CRIME_PUNISHMENT headlines cluster in balkans, canada, iran_opposition, and south_africa, and Meduza's state-TV accountability headline is the clearest example of a Russian-outlet SECURITY_DEFENSE frame collapsing into PUBLIC_OPINION. The Anadolu hantavirus bleed is the most visible misrouting in today's corpus.
